intro: Your attachment style shapes how you connect, argue, and feel loved. Answer 8 honest questions to discover yours — secure, anxious, avoidant, or fearful-avoidant.

questions.text: When your partner doesn't reply for a while, you…

options.label: Assume they're busy and get on with your day
Start worrying something's wrong between you
Barely notice — you're fine doing your own thing
Feel anxious, then tell yourself you don't care

questions.text: The idea of fully depending on a partner…

options.label: Feels natural — that's what partners are for
Sounds wonderful; you wish you were closer
Makes you uneasy; you'd rather rely on yourself
Both appeals to you and scares you

questions.text: After a disagreement, you tend to…

options.label: Talk it through and move on
Need reassurance that you're still okay
Want space and time alone
Swing between fixing it and shutting down

questions.text: When a relationship gets really close, you…

options.label: Enjoy it and lean in
Feel happy but fear they'll leave
Start to feel a little trapped
Want it but feel an urge to pull back

questions.text: Which feels most true about you in love?

options.label: I'm worthy of love and can give it
I worry I'm 'too much' for people
I'm better off not needing anyone
I'm not sure I can be loved the way I want

questions.text: When your partner needs space, you…

options.label: Respect it without taking it personally
Feel rejected and want to close the gap
Feel a little relieved, honestly
Feel hurt but act like it's fine
Comes fairly easily
You do it a lot, reaching for connection
Feels uncomfortable; you'd rather not
You want to, but often freeze up
Comes naturally once they're consistent
Takes a lot of reassurance
Is hard — you keep your guard up
Is a real struggle even when you want to

secure.title: Secure
secure.description: You're comfortable with closeness and independence in equal measure. You trust once someone is consistent, you say what you need, and you don't spiral when things go quiet.
secure.tip: Your steadiness is a gift — it helps an anxious or avoidant partner feel safe enough to meet you there.

anxious.title: Anxious
anxious.description: You love deeply and crave closeness, but a quiet phone or a distant mood can set off real worry. You give a lot, and you need reassurance back to feel secure.
anxious.tip: Name what you need out loud — most partners want to give it, they just can't read your mind.

avoidant.title: Avoidant
avoidant.description: You value your independence and handle things on your own. Too much closeness can feel like pressure, so you create space to breathe — sometimes more than your partner expects.
avoidant.tip: Letting someone in a little at a time isn't weakness — it's exactly how trust gets built.

fearful.title: Fearful-Avoidant
fearful.description: You want closeness and fear it at the same time, so you can run hot then cold. Underneath the push-pull is a real longing to feel safe with someone.
fearful.tip: Slow, consistent reassurance — from both of you — is what calms the push-pull over time.
